documentedOmissions.reason: TL-21 carries the shell runtime split, sandbox preflight, and existing AgenC exec_command boundary integration. The broader shell handler lifecycle remains in runtime/src/tools/system/* and runtime/src/tools/orchestrator.ts rather than being relocated into tools/runtimes.

TL-21 carries unified-exec command extraction plus selected sandbox profile threading into runtime/src/unified-exec/process-manager.ts. Handler formatting, session polling, and write_stdin tool shape remain in the existing AgenC unified-exec and system tool modules.
TL-21 carries apply-patch write target extraction for runtime sandbox enforcement. Patch parsing/application and permission prompts remain owned by runtime/src/tools/apply-patch/*.
Agent and delegation handlers do not define the runtime split being ported in TL-21; they stay in their existing AgenC agent/task tool owners.
Goal, planning, permission request, plugin install, and elicitation handlers are control-surface tools outside the TL-21 runtime split. Their live AgenC owners are not relocated by this item.
Filesystem listing, MCP, image, and test-sync handlers are non-shell handler families. TL-21 only inserts shared runtime scheduling/context/sandbox enforcement before handler execution.
Dynamic discovery, tool search, and unavailable-tool surfaces remain registry/tool-selection concerns, not runtime execution backends.
Donor handler test files are tracked as behavioral references only. Executable coverage for this item is in the AgenC test files listed under checks.
The AgenC registry is already implemented in runtime/src/tool-registry.ts and is consumed by ToolRouter; this item does not relocate the registry.
AgenC event emission is already owned by session/event-log and tool execution hooks; TL-21 preserves those owners.
AgenC tool invocation context already lives in runtime/src/tools/context.ts; TL-21 adds only the runtime-attempt context needed for the split.
